title: B1 Listening - Part 1 - Test 10
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear two students talking before an examination.
items.audio_script: Girl: Are we still meeting in room fourteen for the English examination? I thought I remembered correctly, but I have not checked my messages today. || Boy: That was the original plan, but there are more students taking it than the school expected. [1]We need to meet in the main hall instead[/1]. || Girl: That was lucky; I almost went there. I would have gone to the wrong place and wondered where everyone was. || Boy: Do not worry. I will arrive ten minutes early, so look for me near the entrance. Keep your phone on in case anything changes again.
items.question: Where will the examination take place?
items.explanation: The examination will take place in the main hall.

items.context: You hear two friends preparing for a weekend camp.
items.audio_script: Boy: Have you packed for the weekend camp yet? I put a football in my bag because I thought that was what we needed. || Girl: That was on the first list, but the path to the tents is dark after sunset. [2]Everyone needs to bring a torch now[/2]. || Boy: I am glad I checked. I was about to leave my bag by the door and forget about it. || Girl: There is still time. Put it in now, and check the final message once more before we leave tomorrow morning.
items.question: What should the student bring?
items.explanation: The student should bring a torch.

items.context: You hear two friends deciding what to do tonight.
items.audio_script: Woman: Are we still playing video games at my house this evening? I told my sister I would be home later, so I need to know. || Man: That was the idea, but my uncle has given me two tickets that expire tonight. [3]We are going to see a comedy show instead[/3]. || Woman: That is fine with me. I nearly paid for the other activity online this morning. || Man: It is lucky that you checked first. I will book our places this evening and send you the time. We can meet at the bus stop after lunch.
items.question: What will they do this evening?
items.explanation: They will see a comedy show.

items.context: You hear two students talking about an English class.
items.audio_script: Man: I need to write down the time for the English class tomorrow. Is it still at quarter past nine, like you said yesterday? || Woman: No, the teacher needs to finish earlier for a meeting. [4]It starts at nine o'clock now[/4], although we should arrive ten minutes earlier. || Man: Thanks; I nearly wrote down the wrong time. I would have been waiting there at the wrong time. || Woman: I will send you a reminder later. Bring everything you need, because there will not be time to go home again before it begins.
items.question: What time does the English class start?
items.explanation: The class starts at nine o'clock.

items.context: You hear two people discussing a jacket.
items.audio_script: Girl: How are you getting on with your new jacket? I heard it was not working properly. || Boy: I first thought the sleeves were too short, and my brother wondered if the zip was broken. But he checked it carefully last night. [5]It is not waterproof at all[/5]. || Girl: Can it be fixed before the walking holiday next month? || Boy: I think so. We know what is wrong now, and the repair shop said it should be ready tomorrow afternoon. That is a relief because I need it this weekend.
items.question: What is wrong with the jacket?

items.context: You hear two friends travelling to the theatre.
items.audio_script: Woman: Are you still travelling to the theatre by taxi? I could meet you when you arrive. || Man: I planned to, but it will cost too much in the evening. I considered going by bus, but it takes too long through the traffic. [6]I am going by train instead[/6]. || Woman: That sounds like the best plan. What time will you get there? || Man: Just before eleven, if everything is on time. I have already checked the timetable online, so it should be easy. I will message you when I leave, so you do not have to wait outside.
items.question: How will the speaker travel to the theatre?
items.explanation: The speaker decides to go by train.

items.context: You hear two friends choosing a present for a teacher.
items.audio_script: Man: Did you buy a box of chocolates for Mrs Green's leaving present? I saw one in the shop window yesterday and thought it looked nice. || Woman: No. I looked at a box of chocolates, and I nearly chose some flowers, but she cannot eat chocolate and flowers will not last very long. [7]I bought a photo frame in the end[/7]. || Man: I think that was the right choice. It sounds more useful, and I think it will be appreciated. || Woman: I hope so. I kept the receipt, just in case it needs changing after we give it.
items.question: What present did the speaker buy?
items.explanation: The speaker bought a photo frame.
